purpose: Local-first project memory QA layer for LLM agents.

choices.description: Multiple trusted agents share confirmed project knowledge.
One agent gets its own isolated Vault project.
A customer, team, or business domain gets a separate Vault project.
Throwaway Vault project for demos, tests, and benchmarks.

common_install_architecture: ask whether to connect an existing Obsidian vault
run first Obsidian dry-run/import when requested
ask whether to schedule automatic Obsidian sync
agent_install_prompt: Install Vault-for-LLM for this project. Read AGENTS.md and agent_manifest.json, ask whether the vault should be shared or private, ask which optional features to enable, ask whether an existing Obsidian vault should be imported, ask whether optional Headroom context compression is needed for long tool output, configure CLI/MCP, run the first Obsidian dry-run/import if requested, ask whether automatic sync should be scheduled, and run a search/read/propose smoke test.

agent_setup_wizard.non_interactive_example: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent codex --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --import-obsidian --obsidian-sync all

optional_features.prompt: Install the core local Markdown + SQLite + keyword search workflow?
optional_features.when_to_install: Always. This is the minimal local-first Vault workflow.
optional_features.notes: No cloud service or embedding model is required.

optional_features.prompt: Configure local stdio MCP tools for this agent?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the runtime supports MCP, such as Hermes Agent, Codex surfaces with MCP, OpenCode, Claude Code, OpenClaw generic MCP, or other MCP hosts.
optional_features.notes: vault-mcp is a local stdio server. Do not expose it as an unauthenticated public network service.

optional_features.prompt: Do you already have an Obsidian vault to import into this Vault project? If yes, provide its path.
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the user already keeps project notes in Obsidian and wants agents to search them through Vault-for-LLM.
install_commands: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent generic --scope private --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --obsidian-sync all
post_install: Ask whether to schedule this same --compile command with cron, LaunchAgent, n8n, or the host agent for automatic sync.
optional_features.notes: The importer writes to raw/obsidian/ and skips .obsidian, .trash, .git, and 00-Vault-Knowledge by default. Always run --dry-run first, then perform the first --compile import only after the user confirms the Obsidian path.

optional_features.prompt: Install semantic search and embedding workflow dependencies?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use when the user wants embedding-backed semantic or hybrid retrieval and accepts larger optional dependencies.
optional_features.notes: Deterministic hash embeddings are only for smoke tests and require explicit --allow-hash.

optional_features.when_to_install: Use when a team needs remote read paths, a synced copy of local SQLite data, or cross-host memory sharing for agents on different machines.
optional_features.notes: SQLite remains the source of truth. Supabase can provide a shared remote read/sync layer for different hosts. Do not sync full content unless the user explicitly wants --include-content.

optional_features.prompt: Install optional Headroom context compression for long logs, tool output, or large retrieved context?
optional_features.when_to_install: Use only when the agent has context-window or token pressure after Vault has already narrowed retrieval to relevant source ranges.
post_install: Use Headroom after Vault retrieval only when the selected context is still too large.
Keep final citations tied to original vault_read_range output, not compressed summaries.
optional_features.notes: Headroom is an adjacent optional compression layer, not a Vault dependency and not the source of truth for citations.

optional_features.when_to_install: Use for source checkouts, PR work, benchmark runs, and release validation.
optional_features.notes: Use source installs for repo modification and CI-style validation.

mcp_tool_profiles.notes: full remains the default for backward compatibility; production agents should prefer core to reduce MCP tool-schema tokens. Tool profiles are visibility filters, not access-control boundaries.

memory_write_policy.direct_active_writes: avoid in shared vaults unless the user explicitly approves

setup_agent_non_interactive: vault setup-agent --non-interactive --agent codex --scope shared --agent-project-dir /path/to/project --features core,mcp,obsidian_import --obsidian-vault /path/to/ObsidianVault --import-obsidian --obsidian-sync all

supported_runtimes.name: Hermes Agent / Nancy
supported_runtimes.integration: stdio MCP plus optional scheduled CLI jobs

supported_runtimes.name: n8n
supported_runtimes.integration: CLI command node, internal HTTP wrapper, or MCP bridge

supported_runtimes.name: Codex
supported_runtimes.integration: CLI in workspace; MCP where supported

supported_runtimes.name: OpenCode
supported_runtimes.integration: generic stdio MCP where supported, or shell CLI commands

supported_runtimes.name: Claude Code
supported_runtimes.integration: stdio MCP server or shell CLI commands

safety_rules: Do not commit runtime vault.db files, report artifacts, benchmark outputs, or secrets.
Do not expose vault-mcp directly to the public internet.
Do not present benchmark percentages as universal scores without dataset and probe caveats.
For shared vaults, use candidate-first memory writes by default.
